description: Sculptor Constantin Brâncuși made graceful works that found beauty in the simplification of forms. He created his first “sleeping head” in 1907 and spent the next 20 years on the subject. Here, the tapered, egg-shaped head is given relaxed features created through smooth, curved lines. Its lack of a base suggests, on one hand, that the sculpture may have toppled over; on the other, it shows the muse as if in both physical and metaphorical rest.
title: Sleeping Muse
